config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is how abuse of power starts. In my view, a healthy society protects people even when the majority wants something different, especially for minorities and unpopular voices. Social interests can limit rights in narrow cases like public safety, but the burden should always be on society to justify that limit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika kami tahu betul bahwa mayoritas bisa salah dan negara bisa menyalahgunakan kekuasaan. Hak individu harus menjadi garis dasar, lalu dibatasi hanya kalau ada alasan yang sangat kuat dan jelas, seperti keselamatan publik yang nyata, bukan sekadar tekanan sosial atau kepentingan politik. Kalau hak pribadi selalu dianggap nomor dua, minoritas dan suara yang tidak populer akan paling mudah dikorbankan. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi individu dari keinginan massa, bukan dengan menelannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because that is exactly how people get sacrificed for vague ideas of order or unity. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why collective interests feel important, especially in a country that values harmony and social responsibility, but I also know how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or mistreat minorities. Society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only limits them in clear, necessary, and tightly justified cases like real public safety.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, membungkam minoritas, dan mengabaikan orang yang tidak populer. Hak individu harus menjadi dasar, dan pembatasan hanya sah bila ada alasan yang sangat jelas, sempit, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas menginginkannya. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling sering mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu bahkan ketika itu tidak nyaman bagi kelompok besar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “public interest” can become a cover for silencing dissent or pressuring minorities. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; real public safety, disaster response, and order can justify narrow limits, but those limits must be specific and accountable. The problem is that too many people use the language of harmony or unity to demand obedience, and I do not accept that as a good enough reason. A strong society is not one that asks individuals to disappear, but one that can protect both the community and the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat begitu saja. Dari pengalaman nilai di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan itu sering paling merugikan minoritas serta orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang bisa membatasi hak dalam situasi yang sangat sempit, seperti keselamatan publik yang nyata, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar dibuktikan, bukan sekadar didorong oleh mayoritas. Masyarakat yang sehat justru diukur dari kemampuannya melindungi individu saat itu tidak nyaman bagi kelompok besar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
